Genes.Additional_Notes: Del encodes a bHLH TF that interacts with Ros1 (MYB) to induce anthocyanin biosynthesis. The combination activates a broader spectrum of pathway genes than maize Lc/C1 (which failed to produce anthocyanins in tomato). Del/Ros1 specifically activated F3'5'H (absent with Lc/C1), enabling dihydromyricetin formation required by tomato DFR. Anthocyanin levels comparable to blackberries/blueberries. Trp53-/- mice fed purple tomato diet showed significantly extended lifespan (182.2 ± 8.6 d vs 142.0 ± 8.7 d standard diet; P<0.05).
Genes.Biosynthesis_vs_Catabolism: Biosynthesis (transcriptional activation of the entire anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway)
Genes.Cellular_Compartment: Nucleus (transcriptional regulator); target products accumulate in vacuole
Genes.Control_Mode: Regulatory TF — co-expressed with Ros1, activates nearly all anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including PAL, CHS, CHI, F3H, F3'H, F3'5'H, DFR, ANS, 3-GT, RT, AAC, 5-GT, GST, and a putative anthocyanin transporter (PAT)
Genes.Effect_Direction: Overexpression of Del (with Ros1) → dramatic increase in anthocyanin accumulation in both peel and flesh
Genes.Evidence_Figures_Tables: Figure 1b-d (fruit phenotype), Figure 2a-f (HPLC anthocyanin/flavonoid profiles), Figure 3a (northern blots), Figure 3c (PAL activity), Figure 3d (TEAC antioxidant capacity), Supplementary Fig. 2a-c, Supplementary Tables 1-2
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation (transgenic overexpression) + metabolite quantification (HPLC, spectrophotometry) + enzyme activity assay + northern blot/microarray gene expression + suppression subtractive hybridization
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Anthocyanins (primarily delphinidin and petunidin 3,5-diglucosides acylated with cinnamic acids); also increased flavonols (rutin, myricetin-rhamnoglucoside, kaempferol-rhamnoglucoside) in peel
Genes.Genotype_Lines: Del/Ros1 lines C, N, Y, Z (T0, T1, T2 generations); also transferred to Money Maker, Ailsa Craig, VF36 backgrounds
Genes.Intermediate_Metabolites_Affected: Increased flux through entire phenylpropanoid pathway; increased naringenin chalcone precursors, dihydromyricetin (via F3'5'H activation)
Genes.Measurement_Type: Spectrophotometric quantification of total anthocyanins; HPLC with PDA and ESI-Q-TOF MS/MS for identification; PAL enzyme activity assay; TEAC antioxidant assay
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Overexpression (transgenic); fruit-specific expression driven by E8 promoter in tomato (Solanum lycopersicum cv. MicroTom)
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Line N: 2.83 ± 0.46 mg anthocyanin per g fresh weight (vs. virtually undetectable in wild-type); PAL enzyme activity increased 200-fold in line N; CHI activity increased 3-fold transiently; hydrophilic antioxidant capacity (TEAC) increased 3-fold in line N
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Fruit — pigmentation onset at end of mature green stage, intensifying through ripening; analyzed at ripe stage

Genes.Additional_Notes: Ros1 encodes a MYB TF that interacts with Del (bHLH) to activate anthocyanin biosynthesis. The MYB+bHLH combination gives much higher gene induction throughout all fruit tissues than either factor alone. Ros1/Del activated PAL (rate-determining step in phenylpropanoid metabolism in Solanaceae), CHI (limiting flux in tomato skin flavonoid metabolism), and F3'5'H (required for DFR substrate specificity in tomato), explaining success over Lc/C1.
Genes.Biosynthesis_vs_Catabolism: Biosynthesis (transcriptional activation of the entire anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway)
Genes.Cellular_Compartment: Nucleus (transcriptional regulator); target products accumulate in vacuole
Genes.Control_Mode: Regulatory TF — co-expressed with Del, activates nearly all anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including PAL, 4CL, C4H, C3H, CHS, CHI, F3H, F3'H, F3'5'H, DFR, ANS, 3-GT, RT, AAC, 5-GT, GST, and PAT
Genes.Effect_Direction: Overexpression of Ros1 (with Del) → dramatic increase in anthocyanin accumulation in both peel and flesh
Genes.Evidence_Figures_Tables: Figure 1b-d, Figure 2a-f, Figure 3a-d, Supplementary Figs. 2-4, Supplementary Tables 1-2
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation (transgenic overexpression) + metabolite quantification + enzyme activity assay + northern blot/microarray + suppression subtractive hybridization
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Anthocyanins (primarily delphinidin and petunidin 3,5-diglucosides acylated with cinnamic acids); also increased flavonols (rutin, myricetin derivatives, kaempferol derivatives) in peel
Genes.Genotype_Lines: Del/Ros1 lines C, N, Y, Z (T0, T1, T2 generations); also transferred to Money Maker, Ailsa Craig, VF36 backgrounds
Genes.Intermediate_Metabolites_Affected: Increased flux through entire phenylpropanoid pathway; activation of F3'5'H enables dihydromyricetin formation as intermediate for delphinidin-based anthocyanins
Genes.Measurement_Type: Spectrophotometric quantification; HPLC-PDA-ESI-Q-TOF MS/MS; enzyme activity assays; TEAC antioxidant assay
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Overexpression (transgenic); fruit-specific expression driven by E8 promoter in tomato (Solanum lycopersicum cv. MicroTom)
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Line N: 2.83 ± 0.46 mg anthocyanin per g fresh weight (vs. virtually undetectable in wild-type); PAL enzyme activity increased 200-fold; CHI activity increased 3-fold; hydrophilic TEAC increased 3-fold
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Fruit — onset at end of mature green stage through ripening; analyzed at ripe stage

Title: Enrichment of tomato fruit with health-promoting anthocyanins by expression of select transcription factors
